finding_results.notes: The midsection is now broken by the uneasy meal, Bond's earlier warning, Loid's fight with keeping Anya off the floor, and the Damian trust test. Operational detail remains, but it stays tied to family strain and social cost instead of running as a clean logistics block.
The chapter now states the three-day gap in the opening sentence and reinforces the Tuesday-night timing in the first paragraph.
Revised chapter length verified at 6832 words while preserving the heading contract.

Replaced Sylvia's remembered death notification from verbatim calibration text ('Agent Crane is dead') to institutional WISE shorthand ('We lost Crane at the checkpoint'), preserving her flat professional register without copying the example line. Replaced Anya's spoken disclosure from the echoing 'Crane is dead' to 'They killed Crane', shifting to active voice with angrier pressure-true bluntness appropriate to Anya's family register under duress. A third instance of 'Crane is dead' at line 515 (Damian's interior thought) was left untouched because it is Damian's own internal cataloging voice, not a reproduction of Sylvia's institutional register or the calibration pattern.
Recast the packet-targeted clipped narration beats as complete sentences while preserving the same close-third pressure and scene function.
Reduced chapter-level 'old dog' repetition to the single earned age-versus-urgency line at 423; adjacent Bond beats now rely on behavior and naming instead of re-certifying his age.
Rephrased the telepathic-consent violation in theft/trespass terms rather than the repeated 'offered' frame, while also removing the stray process-leak phrasing on that sentence.

pass_summaries.summary: Expanded Chapter 14 into an on-page family reckoning and contested two-stage planning sequence. The revision now makes the time jump explicit, dramatizes Anya's selective disclosure and Damian's earned inclusion, preserves Yor's Garden break as a quiet decisive turn, moves Bond's warning earlier to keep pressure alive, and clears the minimum word-count target without changing the chapter-end state.

Single finding addressed: style-bible calibration language ('Agent Crane is dead') removed from both the remembered Sylvia notification (line 3) and Anya's spoken disclosure (line 33). Sylvia's beat now uses 'We lost Crane at the checkpoint' (institutional collective-loss framing). Anya's beat now uses 'They killed Crane' (active, angrier, character-true under pressure). No structural, continuity, or voice-signature collateral changes. Heading contract preserved.

All three prose/copyedit findings for chapter 14 were resolved: the clipped narration beats now read as complete sentences, Bond's 'old dog' wording appears only once in the chapter, and the telepathic violation language now varies away from the repeated 'offered' construction.
